action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py fails because torch.load default weights_only=True can't handle numpy arrays saved in checkpoint (train_mean, train_std). Analysis: Our checkpoint includes numpy arrays alongside model state_dict, which triggers the error. Fix: add weights_only=False to torch.load in predict.py. Also need to ensure model loads correctly. Decision: fix predict.py, retrain with slight capacity increase to improve baseline, then submit eval.
